flashcards.front: What is an array in data structures?
flashcards.back: An array is a collection of elements of the same data type stored in contiguous memory locations, accessible randomly using an index.

flashcards.front: What is the starting index of an array in most programming languages?
flashcards.back: In C, C++, Java, and Python, array indices start from 0 (zero-based indexing).

flashcards.front: What is the main advantage of storing array elements in contiguous memory locations?
flashcards.back: Contiguous memory allocation enables O(1) constant time access to any element using the index, making random access very efficient.

flashcards.front: What is a key disadvantage of static arrays?
flashcards.back: Static arrays have fixed size declared at compile time, leading to memory wastage if underutilized or overflow if more space is needed.

flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of accessing an element in an array by index?
flashcards.back: O(1) - constant time complexity because the address of any element can be calculated directly using the formula: address = base + (index × element_size).

flashcards.front: How does a 2D array differ from a 1D array in memory representation?
flashcards.back: A 2D array can be stored in row-major order (consecutive rows) or column-major order (consecutive columns), forming a matrix-like structure.

flashcards.front: What is row-major order in 2D arrays?
flashcards.back: In row-major order, elements of each row are stored consecutively in memory. The element at arr[i][j] is at index (i × n + j) for an n-column matrix.
A dynamic array (like ArrayList in Java, list in Python) can resize itself during runtime, typically doubling in size when full to achieve amortized O(1) insertion.

flashcards.front: How do arrays differ from linked lists in terms of memory allocation?
flashcards.back: Arrays use contiguous memory allocation (static or dynamic), while linked lists use non-contiguous memory with nodes linked via pointers.
A sparse matrix is a matrix where most elements are zero. It is efficiently stored using arrays to store only non-zero elements with their row and column positions.

flashcards.front: How can polynomials be represented using arrays?
flashcards.back: Polynomials can be represented using arrays where the array index represents the exponent and the value at that index represents the coefficient.
A jagged array is an array of arrays where each inner array can have different lengths (unlike a rectangular 2D array).

flashcards.front: How is the address of an element calculated in a 1D array?
flashcards.back: Address of arr[i] = Base Address + (i × Element Size), where i is the index and element size is bytes per element.

flashcards.front: What are the bounds of an array declaration?
flashcards.back: Lower bound is the starting index (usually 0), and upper bound is the last valid index (n-1 for n elements). Array indices must fall within these bounds.

flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of inserting an element at the beginning of an array?
flashcards.back: O(n) - linear time because all existing elements must be shifted one position to the right to make space at index 0.
